text: Traditional dependency parsers rely on millions of sparse indicator features and the features tend to be weak and computationally expensive to extract. In this paper, we propose a new neural network classifier for greedy, transition-based dependency parsing. The parser uses a small set of dense features which lead to a large gain in both speed and accuracy. The experiments are conducted on both the English Wall Street Journal (WSJ) and the Chinese Penn Treebank (CTB) datasets and the gain in LAS and UAS is around 2%. Our parser is very efficient. It parses over 1,000 sentences per second with an UAS of 92.2% on the WSJ dataset.
explanation: This reads like a technical abstract with concrete metrics and datasets, a style common to both human-written and AI-generated scientific text. The neutral tone and standard phrasing make it hard to distinguish AI origin with certainty.
